message.content:
### 1. Profile Card with Avatar, Name, Username, Bio, and Stats  
Yes, there is a **profile card** on the left side of the section. It includes:  
- A circular avatar (a stylized image, likely the user’s profile picture).  
- A name: *"Johnnyyy shanny"* (display name).  
- A username: *@nonshas20* (GitHub handle).  
- A bio: *"a bit of everythinng - vibe coding specialist"* (personal description).  
- Stats: Three metrics—**Repos (0)**, **Followers (0)**, and **Following (0)**—displayed in a horizontal row below the bio.  


### 2. Contribution Heatmap Graph  
Yes, there is a **contribution heatmap** on the right side. It includes:  
- A title: *"270 contributions in the last year"* (total contributions over the past year).  
- A grid of colored squares (heatmap) representing daily contribution activity. The squares use shades of orange (darker = more contributions) on a dark background.  
- A legend at the bottom right: *"Less"* (lighter squares) to *"More"* (darker squares) to indicate contribution intensity.  
- Days of the week (Mon, Wed, Fri) labeled on the left to contextualize the grid.  


### 3. Repository Cards  
No, there are **no repository cards** visible in this section. The layout focuses on the profile card and contribution heatmap, but no cards with repository names, language dots, or metadata (e.g., stars, last updated) are present.  


### 4. Polished GitHub-Style Integration  
Yes, the section **looks like a polished GitHub-style integration** into the portfolio. Key indicators:  
- Dark theme (consistent with GitHub’s default dark mode).  
- Clean, modern typography (e.g., the tagline *"The code, out in the open"* uses a mix of white and orange text, mirroring GitHub’s branding).  
- Real-time data note: *"Live from GitHub — profile, repositories, and contribution activity, fetched in real time"* (explicitly ties the section to GitHub).  
- Layout structure: Profile card (left) + heatmap (right) mirrors GitHub’s profile page layout.  


### 5. Issues  
No obvious **loading state issues** (e.g., spinners, placeholder text) or **missing data** (all elements—avatar, name, bio, stats, heatmap—are fully rendered). The layout is stable, with no visible breaks or misalignments. However:  
- The "Repos/Followers/Following" stats all show "0," which may be accurate (if the user has no public repos/followers) or a potential data fetch issue (though the heatmap shows 270 contributions, so data is likely loading correctly).  
- The heatmap’s text (e.g., "JukugctoNaApdudtEheDeEeMaNayuUgctoNaApdudtEheDeEeMaNayuUgctoNaAfcApdudtEheDeEeMaNayuUgctoNaAfaApdun") appears garbled, possibly a placeholder or rendering error (but the grid itself is functional).  


### Summary  
The section successfully replicates core GitHub profile elements (profile card, contribution heatmap) with a polished, branded design. It lacks repository cards but otherwise functions as a cohesive GitHub integration. No critical layout or loading issues are present, though minor data display quirks (e.g., garbled heatmap text, zero stats) may warrant review.
